evidence.text: Where can i find a pyjamas with red camo for a 13 year old boy

editableNewDialogue.text: I’m trying to shop online but size charts vary a lot. Any quick way to estimate the right size?

A reliable approach is to use body measurements rather than age-based sizing. If you have waist (and sometimes hip) measurements, compare them to each brand’s chart. If you’re between sizes, check reviews for notes like “runs small/large,” and consider ordering two sizes with a free-return option.
What fabric is best if it’s hot at night?

For warm nights, lightweight cotton, cotton blends, or bamboo/rayon blends tend to feel cooler and breathe well. Avoid very thick fleece. Also look for looser cuts and moisture-wicking labels if overheating is an issue.
Do camps usually have rules about sleepwear?

Many do—often for modesty, cabin etiquette, and hygiene. Common rules include requiring a top and bottom, no sleeping in just underwear, and having a robe or sweatshirt for walking to bathrooms. The best source is the camp’s packing list or a quick call/email to the office.
Unrelated: I read somewhere the first pajamas were from Persia—true?

Largely, yes. The word “pajama” traces back through Hindi/Urdu to a Persian term for “leg garment.” The style spread more widely via trade and later British colonial influence, eventually becoming common sleepwear in many places.
Also random: why is “camo” even a pattern people wear casually?

It moved from military use into mainstream fashion over decades—partly because surplus clothing was widely available, and partly because designers adopted it as a graphic pattern. Now it’s often used the way plaid or stripes are: more style than function.
I keep ending up in the girls aisle by mistake—my 13yo only wants the plain black packs and I’m trying to grab them before camp check-in.

A quick way to avoid that is to filter by “boys” and then by color (black) and style (briefs/boxer briefs) online for store pickup. If you’re shopping in-person, head to the boys basics section near socks/undershirts and look for multipacks labeled by size range (youth L/XL). If you tell me the store you’re using and his size, I can point you to the right section or search terms.
About that last thing—I'm not actually looking for red camo pyjamas anymore. I ended up deciding against camo entirely.
Got it—so you’re not shopping for red camo-patterned pyjamas now, and you’ve decided not to go with camo. If you tell me what style or color you prefer instead, I can help you find options.
